text:
🔭 Opus 4.8 workflows for research / scraping in Personal OS

TL;DR: Dynamic workflows auto-generate a JS orchestration script spinning up parallel subagents (up to 1,000) — a Claude Code Enterprise/Max feature, not a general API pattern. For the Personal OS they're overkill at routine scale but worth trialing for heavy multi-source research (complex explore briefs, market sweeps). NOT a fit for web scraping, where the bottleneck is rate-limiting not parallelism. Current sequential skill composition stays the right default for bounded tasks.

Key points:
· Triggered by the word "workflow" in a Claude Code prompt or /effort ultracode; requires Claude Code ≥ v2.1.154 + Enterprise/Team/Max plan (research preview, May 2026) [Fact]
· Plan lives in a JS script + script variables — not Claude's context — so context holds only the final answer; 16 concurrent agents, 1,000 per run cap [Fact]
· Anthropic internal research eval: multi-agent Opus scores 90.2% better than single-agent — but methodology undisclosed [Practitioner claim]
· Cost: each agent carries its own context overhead — hundreds of agents = orders-of-magnitude more tokens than a standard session [Practitioner consensus]
